senators.name: Adam Schiff
senators.nerdReason: B.A. from Stanford University and J.D. from Harvard Law School. Career as federal prosecutor and long-tenured House Intelligence Committee chair. Led multiple high-profile investigations and both Trump impeachment proceedings as evidence-marshaling lead. Combines elite legal training with deep intelligence and national security analytical expertise. Known for meticulous, evidence-based case-building in oversight and law enforcement contexts.

senators.name: Alex Padilla
senators.nerdReason: B.S. in mechanical engineering from MIT. Career as engineer before politics. MIT engineering degree is among the most rigorous STEM educations available. Former California Secretary of State where he led evidence-based election security and cybersecurity initiatives. One of the very few senators with an engineering degree from a world-class institution. Applies technical expertise to voting rights, cybersecurity, and immigration policy.

CA-02.name: Jared Huffman
CA-02.nerdReason: B.A. from UC Santa Barbara and J.D. from Boston College Law School. Career as environmental attorney. Deep policy expertise in water, public lands, and climate grounded in environmental law research. Serves on House Natural Resources Committee. Known for evidence-based approach to conservation and climate policy.

CA-03.name: Kevin Kiley
CA-03.notNerdReason: B.A. from Harvard University, J.D. from Yale Law School, and M.Ed. from Harvard Graduate School of Education. Three elite degrees. Teacher and attorney. Despite exceptional credentials, legislative approach is primarily ideologically driven rather than evidence-based. Focused on opposing California education policy rather than constructive research-grounded reform.
education: M.Ed., Harvard Graduate School of Education

CA-04.name: Mike Thompson
CA-04.notNerdReason: B.A. and M.A. from California State University, Chico. Career in state legislature. Vietnam veteran. Winemaker. Deep expertise in gun violence prevention and agriculture policy. Solid policy knowledge but approach not distinctly analytical or research-driven.

CA-05.name: Tom McClintock
CA-05.notNerdReason: B.A. from UCLA. Career in California state legislature. Fiscal conservative known for budgetary analysis. Deep knowledge of fiscal and water policy but approach is ideologically driven rather than evidence-based.

CA-06.name: Ami Bera
CA-06.nerdReason: B.A. from UC Irvine and M.D. from UC Irvine School of Medicine. Practiced as a physician and served as Chief Medical Officer for Sacramento County. Applying medical expertise to healthcare and global health policy. Serves on House Foreign Affairs Committee. Career at the intersection of medicine and policy demonstrates evidence-based analytical approach.
education: M.D., UC Irvine School of Medicine

CA-07.name: Doris Matsui
CA-07.notNerdReason: B.A. from UC Berkeley. Career as White House aide and congresswoman. Long-tenured on Energy and Commerce Committee. Deep policy expertise in technology, broadband, and telecommunications but approach not distinctly analytical.
experience: White House Deputy Assistant to President Clinton

CA-08.name: John Garamendi
CA-08.notNerdReason: B.A. from UC Berkeley and M.B.A. from Harvard Business School. Career as businessman, California state legislator, and Lieutenant Governor. Harvard MBA provides quantitative business training. Deep expertise in transportation and defense policy. Solid credentials but approach not distinctly evidence-based.

CA-09.name: Josh Harder
CA-09.nerdReason: B.A. from Yale University and M.B.A. from Stanford Graduate School of Business. Career in venture capital before Congress. Yale + Stanford GSB represents elite analytical training. Applies investment and financial analytical expertise to healthcare, agriculture, and economic development policy.
education: M.B.A., Stanford Graduate School of Business

CA-10.name: Mark DeSaulnier
CA-10.notNerdReason: B.A. from Holy Cross College. Career in restaurant management, California state legislature, and Congress. Deep expertise in transportation and workplace safety. Long legislative tenure but approach not distinctly analytical.
experience: Contra Costa County Board of Supervisors

CA-11.name: Nancy Pelosi
CA-11.notNerdReason: B.A. in political science from Trinity College. Former Speaker of the House (first woman to hold the position). Extraordinary legislative acumen and strategic intelligence. Passed the Affordable Care Act, CHIPS and Science Act, and Inflation Reduction Act. Her analytical approach is political-strategic rather than research-driven — but mastery of legislative procedure and policy detail is exceptional.

CA-12.name: Lateefah Simon
CA-12.notNerdReason: Did not complete a college degree. Career in criminal justice reform advocacy, BART Board of Directors, and social justice organizing. Deep expertise in criminal justice and transportation policy driven by lived experience and community activism rather than analytical frameworks.
experience: Executive Director, Lawyers Committee for Civil Rights

CA-13.name: Adam Gray
CA-13.notNerdReason: B.A. from UCLA. Career in California state legislature. No analytical or evidence-based policy background.

CA-14.name: Eric Swalwell
CA-14.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of Maryland and J.D. from McGeorge School of Law. Career as prosecutor and state legislator. House Intelligence Committee. Legal and prosecutorial background provides analytical skills but governance approach not distinctly research-driven.

CA-15.name: Kevin Mullin
CA-15.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of San Francisco. Career in California state legislature. No analytical or evidence-based policy background.

CA-16.name: Sam Liccardo
CA-16.nerdReason: B.A. from Harvard University and J.D. from Santa Clara University School of Law. Career as attorney and 8-year mayor of San Jose — the largest tech hub city in the world. Harvard education + mayoral leadership of Silicon Valley required deep data-driven governance on housing, transportation, technology, and policing. Applies analytical urban governance expertise to federal policy.
education: J.D., Santa Clara University School of Law

CA-17.name: Ro Khanna
CA-17.nerdReason: B.A. from University of Chicago and J.D. from Yale Law School. Adjunct Professor of Economics and Law at Stanford University. Former Deputy Assistant Secretary of Commerce for Domestic Operations under Obama. Combines elite legal training with economics expertise and Stanford faculty engagement. Deep tech policy expertise in Silicon Valley. Authored 'Dignity in a Digital Age' on the economics of technology and work. One of Congress's foremost technology policy experts.

CA-18.name: Zoe Lofgren
CA-18.nerdReason: B.A. from Stanford University and J.D. from Santa Clara University School of Law. Long-tenured member with deep immigration law expertise. Former Chair of the House Administration Committee overseeing federal election administration. Stanford education provides analytical foundation. Known for detailed, evidence-based work on immigration, election law, and technology policy.
education: J.D., Santa Clara University School of Law

CA-19.name: Jimmy Panetta
CA-19.notNerdReason: B.A. from UC Davis and J.D. from Santa Clara University School of Law. Career as attorney and Navy JAG officer (prosecutor). Son of former CIA Director and Defense Secretary Leon Panetta. Legal and military background. Solid credentials but governance approach not distinctly evidence-based.
education: J.D., Santa Clara University School of Law

CA-20.name: Vince Fong
CA-20.notNerdReason: B.A. from UC Berkeley and M.P.P. from Harvard Kennedy School. Career as congressional staffer and California state assemblyman. Harvard Kennedy School policy analysis training shows. But new member with limited legislative record to assess evidence-based governance.
experience: Chief of Staff to Rep. Kevin McCarthy

CA-21.name: Jim Costa
CA-21.notNerdReason: B.A. from Fresno State University. Career as farmer, state legislator, and congressman. Long tenure on Agriculture Committee with deep expertise in water, farming, and Central Valley issues. Practical agricultural knowledge but approach not analytically rigorous.

CA-22.name: David Valadao
CA-22.notNerdReason: No college degree. Career as dairy farmer and state legislator. Voted to impeach Trump in 2021. Agricultural and business background. No academic credentials or evidence-based policy approach.

CA-23.name: Jay Obernolte
CA-23.nerdReason: B.S. in engineering and applied science from California Institute of Technology (Caltech) and M.S. in artificial intelligence from UCLA. Career as video game developer (founded FarSight Studios, which created hundreds of titles). Caltech is one of the world's premier STEM institutions. Masters in AI from UCLA is cutting-edge technical education. One of the most technically credentialed members of Congress. Applies genuine tech and AI expertise to technology policy.
education: B.S. Engineering and Applied Science, California Institute of Technology (Caltech)

CA-24.name: Salud Carbajal
CA-24.notNerdReason: B.A. from UC Santa Barbara and M.P.A. from University of Southern California. Career as community organizer and county supervisor. Marine Corps Reserve veteran. MPA provides policy analysis training. Deep engagement on veterans issues and environment but approach not distinctly analytical.
experience: Santa Barbara County Board of Supervisors

CA-25.name: Raul Ruiz
CA-25.nerdReason: B.S. from UCLA, M.D. from Harvard Medical School, M.P.H. from Harvard School of Public Health, and M.P.P. from Harvard Kennedy School. Four Harvard degrees — an extraordinary academic achievement. Emergency medicine physician. Founder of nonprofit medical clinics. One of the most academically credentialed members of Congress. Deep scientific and public health expertise applied to healthcare, water, and desert community policy.
education: M.P.H., Harvard School of Public Health

CA-26.name: Julia Brownley
CA-26.notNerdReason: B.A. from Mount Vernon College and M.B.A. from American University. Career in education and California state legislature. MBA provides analytical training. Deep expertise in veterans, transportation, and education. Solid but approach not distinctly research-driven.

CA-27.name: George Whitesides
CA-27.nerdReason: B.S. from Williams College and M.Sc. from Oxford University as a Rhodes Scholar. Career as NASA Chief of Staff and deputy administrator of the FAA Office of Commercial Space Transportation. Deeply embedded in aerospace, space exploration, and aviation administration — technical fields requiring strong STEM analytical skills. Applies genuine aerospace policy expertise to Congress.
experience: Deputy Administrator, FAA Office of Commercial Space

CA-28.name: Judy Chu
CA-28.nerdReason: B.A. in mathematics from UCLA and Ph.D. in psychology from California School of Professional Psychology. Doctorate in psychology with a mathematics undergraduate degree represents strong analytical training. Career as psychologist, professor, and state legislator. First Chinese American woman elected to Congress. Applies research expertise to healthcare, mental health, and small business policy.
education: Ph.D. Psychology, California School of Professional Psychology
experience: Chair, Congressional Asian Pacific American Caucus

CA-29.name: Luz Rivas
CA-29.notNerdReason: B.S. from MIT and career as an electrical engineer and founder of Arleta STEAM Center (STEM nonprofit for youth). MIT engineering degree is exceptional. However, her primary focus in Congress is community-based education advocacy rather than technical policy, and her legislative record is too new to assess fully.

CA-30.name: Laura Friedman
CA-30.notNerdReason: B.A. from Northwestern University and M.A. from UCLA Film School. Career in entertainment business and California state legislature. Deep expertise in transportation and environment policy. California Assembly Transportation Committee chair. Solid but approach not distinctly analytical.

CA-31.name: Gil Cisneros
CA-31.notNerdReason: B.A. from George Washington University and M.S. from Navy Postgraduate School. U.S. Navy officer. Philanthropist (lottery winner who donated to education). Military and education philanthropy background. No distinctly analytical policy approach.

CA-32.name: Brad Sherman
CA-32.nerdReason: B.A. from UCLA (magna cum laude) and J.D. from Harvard Law School. Holds a Certified Public Accountant (CPA) license — passed the CPA exam while at Harvard. Career as tax attorney and California state Board of Equalization. Deep expertise in tax law, financial regulation, and nuclear nonproliferation. CPA + Harvard Law provides exceptional quantitative and analytical credentials applied directly to fiscal and foreign policy.

CA-33.name: Pete Aguilar
CA-33.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of Redlands. Career as mayor of Redlands and state legislator. House Democratic Caucus Chair. No analytical or research-based policy background.

CA-34.name: Jimmy Gomez
CA-34.notNerdReason: B.A. from UCLA and M.P.P. from Harvard Kennedy School. Career as community organizer and California state legislator. Harvard Kennedy School policy analysis training. Deep policy engagement on healthcare and economy but approach not distinctly research-driven.

CA-35.name: Norma Torres
CA-35.notNerdReason: Career as 911 dispatcher, state legislator, and congresswoman. No college degree. Deep expertise in public safety and labor policy grounded in personal experience as a 911 dispatcher. No analytical or research-based policy approach.

CA-36.name: Ted Lieu
CA-36.nerdReason: B.S. in computer science from Stanford University and J.D. from Georgetown University Law Center. U.S. Air Force JAG officer. One of the few members of Congress with a computer science degree from Stanford. Applies tech expertise to AI regulation, cybersecurity, and privacy policy. Co-chairs the Bipartisan AI Task Force. Known for technically informed technology policy advocacy.

CA-37.name: Sydney Kamlager-Dove
CA-37.notNerdReason: B.A. from Spelman College and M.F.A. from University of Southern California. Career in theater arts, state legislature, and Congress. Arts and humanities background. No analytical or evidence-based policy approach.

CA-38.name: Linda Sanchez
CA-38.notNerdReason: B.A. from UCLA and J.D. from UC Berkeley School of Law. Career as labor attorney, state legislator, and congresswoman. Berkeley Law provides strong analytical training. Deep expertise in labor, immigration, and Social Security policy. Solid credentials but approach not distinctly research-driven.
education: J.D., UC Berkeley School of Law

CA-39.name: Mark Takano
CA-39.notNerdReason: B.A. from Harvard University and pursued but did not complete a Ph.D. at UC Riverside. Career as history teacher at Rialto High School for 23 years, then congressman. Harvard education and near-PhD shows intellectual engagement. Deep education and veterans policy expertise. Former Chair of House Veterans Affairs Committee. Solid but approach not distinctly analytical.
experience: Former Chair, House Veterans Affairs Committee

CA-40.name: Young Kim
CA-40.notNerdReason: B.A. from USC and M.B.A. from California State University, Fullerton. Career in business (marketing and accounting) and state legislature. MBA provides business analytical skills. First Korean American Republican woman in Congress. Solid credentials but approach not distinctly evidence-based.

CA-41.name: Ken Calvert
CA-41.notNerdReason: B.A. from San Diego State University. Career in real estate and restaurant business, then Congress. Dean of California Republican delegation. Business background. No analytical or evidence-based policy approach.

CA-42.name: Robert Garcia
CA-42.notNerdReason: B.A. from California State University, Long Beach and M.A. from USC Rossier School of Education. Career as higher education administrator, communications executive, and mayor of Long Beach. First openly gay immigrant elected to Congress. Education management background shows some policy analytical engagement. Solid but approach not distinctly research-driven.
education: M.A., USC Rossier School of Education
B.A., California State University, Long Beach
experience: Vice Chancellor, Long Beach Community College

CA-43.name: Maxine Waters
CA-43.notNerdReason: B.A. from California State University, Los Angeles. Career in social work, education, and state legislature before Congress. Longest-serving member of CA delegation. Fierce advocate but approach is activist-driven rather than analytically rigorous.
education: B.A., California State University, Los Angeles
experience: Ranking Member, House Financial Services Committee

CA-44.name: Nanette Barragan
CA-44.notNerdReason: B.A. from UCLA and J.D. from UC Hastings College of the Law. Career as attorney, city council member, and congresswoman. Legal training provides analytical skills. Deep expertise on immigration, healthcare, and workers' rights. Solid but approach not distinctly evidence-based.
education: J.D., UC Hastings College of the Law

CA-45.name: Derek Tran
CA-45.notNerdReason: B.A. from UC Berkeley and J.D. from Loyola Law School. Career as personal injury attorney. UC Berkeley + Loyola Law provides strong educational background. New member with limited legislative record. Legal background shows analytical training.

CA-46.name: Lou Correa
CA-46.notNerdReason: B.A. from CSU Fullerton, J.D. from UCLA, and MBA from Stanford Graduate School of Business. Three degrees including Stanford MBA. Career as attorney, state senator, and Orange County Supervisor. Stanford MBA and UCLA Law show strong credentials but governance approach is more political than analytical.
education: M.B.A., Stanford Graduate School of Business

CA-47.name: Dave Min
CA-47.nerdReason: B.A. from Cornell University and J.D. from Georgetown University Law Center. Career as financial regulation policy attorney at the Center for American Progress and law professor at UC Irvine School of Law. Deep expertise in financial law, securities regulation, and housing finance grounded in academic research. Law professor background demonstrates evidence-based analytical approach to policy.
experience: Law Professor, UC Irvine School of Law
Policy Attorney, Center for American Progress

CA-48.name: Darrell Issa
CA-48.notNerdReason: No college degree. Career as car alarm businessman (DEI, later Directed Electronics). Became a multi-millionaire through tech entrepreneurship. Business instinct and tech industry experience but no academic credentials or evidence-based governance approach.
experience: Founder and CEO, DEI (car alarm company)

CA-49.name: Mike Levin
CA-49.nerdReason: B.A. from Stanford University and J.D. from Duke University School of Law. Career as environmental and energy attorney. Stanford education and Duke Law provides strong analytical foundation. Deep expertise in clean energy policy, nuclear energy, and environmental law grounded in technical understanding of energy systems.
education: J.D., Duke University School of Law
experience: CEO, Democrat Party of Orange County

CA-50.name: Scott Peters
CA-50.nerdReason: B.A. from Duke University and J.D. from NYU School of Law. Career as environmental attorney and San Diego City Council president. Deep expertise in bioscience, innovation, and climate policy built through engagement with San Diego's research and biotech community. Known for methodical, bipartisan approach to drug pricing and science policy.
education: J.D., New York University School of Law

CA-51.name: Sara Jacobs
CA-51.nerdReason: B.A. and M.A. from Columbia University. Career in international development (UNICEF) and humanitarian policy. Columbia provides rigorous analytical liberal arts training. Deep expertise in conflict prevention, child protection, and humanitarian policy grounded in research-backed frameworks from international organizations.

CA-52.name: Juan Vargas
CA-52.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of San Diego, M.A. from Fordham University, and J.D. from Harvard Law School. Harvard Law provides elite analytical training. Career as attorney and state legislator. Despite Harvard credentials, legislative approach is not distinctly research-driven.
